Dwivedy   Rail Nilayam,Secunderabad -500 025  FOREWORD Dear All S&T Stakeholders, •   It gives me immense satisfaction in handing over the South Central Railway Schedule of •   Rates for Signal & Telecommunications - 2024, prepared after comprehensive review of SOR-2012, redrafting of many descriptions of items duly incorporating the relevant RDSO •   specifications and drawings, critically examined by the SAG committee, thoroughly scrutinized by the Accounts member and finally validated by CSE-I before its approval. .   1 hope the revised description of items containing specification and drawings will clear all the ambiguities which might have been present in the SOR-2012 leading to interpretation issues. Similarly, the unit rates arrived after considering the latest Accepted Rates and rate analysis in some cases will give the confidence, to all stakeholders while preparing •   estimates, inviting tenders, quoting rates in the tender, justifying the offered rates during the tender committee proceedings and finally acceptance of the tender. It will not only ease the process of tendering and award of the contract, but also lead to faster execution due to more clarity in the description and specifications of the works to be undertaken, .   and will certainly improve the quality of S&T works, safety at work site and minimum post commissioning issues. I appreciate the hard work done by the JAG officers' •   committee of S&T and Accounts branch involved in preparation of the SOR-2024, SAG officers committee for their valuable inputs in bringing out a more clear, comprehensive •   and logical schedule of rates 2024.  .  •   10th May 2024 Secunderabad  Telephone : 040-27824755, 27786299  I'm confident that this SOR - 2024 will educate and train all stakeholders, including railway supervisors, officers and contractors' supervisors in faster delivery of the quality S&T installation. Any suggestion to further improve this SOR-2024 shall be appreciated.
S  •  •   PREFACE S&T Department of SCR is following schedule of rates prepared in year 2012 which is almost 12 years old. In this era of rapid change and continuous development, it has become imperative to regularly update and refine our financial schedules, to stay aligned with evolving standards, expectations, and market dynamics. The introduction of this New Schedule of Rates (SOR) is an embodiment of our unwavering commitment to transparency, fairness, and efficiency in serving our stakeholders and communities. To standardise and update the Schedule of Rates, PCSTE SCR, nominated a committee of •   three S&T officers (Dy.CSTE/Proj/SC,   Dy.CSTE/C-llISC   and Sr.DSTE/SC) and one Finance Officer(Sr.AFNF)(/HQ/SC). The committee has reviewed the description, specifications and the rates of the SOR items and added /modified several items of SOR to suit the current S&T works requirement. The proposed SOR consists of many new items added with modified •   description / specification under supply portion and under labour portion of the work. In this new SOR, rate analysis is inclusive of 18% GST and also inclusive of Contractor Profit @10%. The initial report prepared by SOR Review Committee in Nov 2021, which has been studied •   by the group of 2 SAG Officers (CSTE/Project-Il & CSTE/Con) and observations were submitted to SOR committee in April 2022. SOR Review Committee after detailed study of the above observations has made necessary changes in the initial report along with item-wise '   compliance on the observations made by the SAG Officers Group and the final SOR was submitted by the committee in Nov 2022. The SAG level group has reviewed the final SOR '   prepared along with item-wise compliance and has agreed for further approval and adoption. PCSTE/SCR has reviewed the final SOR and advised to delete few items & review the SOR. Further SOR was reviewed and the specifications were updated for some of the items. As you navigate through the pages of this schedule, we encourage you to engage with us, '   share your insights, and contribute to the ongoing refinement of our practices. This schedule is dynamic, intended to evolve based on constructive feedback and the changing needs of our environment. We extend our deepest gratitude to everyone who has contributed to the development of this  5   schedule—their expertise, dedication, and insight have been invaluable. To our clients, partners, and the wider community, we pledge our continued commitment to working together •   towards shared goals, driven by this New Schedule of Rates. Performance Guarantee (PG) : The successful bidder shall have to  submit a Performance Guarantee (PG) within 21 (Twenty one)  days from the date of issue of Letter of Acceptance (LOA).  Extension of time for submission of PG beyond 21 (Twenty one)  days and upto 60 days from the date of issue of LOA may be given  by the Authority who is competent to sign the contract agreement.  However, a penal interest of 12% per annum shall be charged for  the delay beyond 21(Twenty one) days, i.e. from 22nd day after the  date of issue of LOA. Further, if the 60th day happens to be a  declared   holiday   in   the   concerned   office   of   the   Railway,  submission of PG can be accepted on the next working day.
a): In all other cases, if the Contractor fails to submit the requisite  PG even after 60 days from the date of issue of LOA, the contract  is liable to be terminated. In case contract is terminated railway  shall be entitled to forfeit Earnest Money Deposit and other dues  payable against that contract.
